title: Methods and nodes for activation or deactivation of a carrier in a communication network supporting carrier aggregation
abstract: A method in a User Equipment (UE) for activation or deactivation of a carrier in a communication network supporting carrier aggregation is provided. The method comprises: receiving a control signal for a transmission of a carrier activation or deactivation message command, the control signal including an indication of a first resource for the UE to transmit a feedback message to a network node; receiving a carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the carrier activation or deactivation message comprises an indication of a second resource for the UE to transmit the feedback message to the network node; decoding the received carrier activation or deactivation message; and sending the feedback message using one of the first resource and the second resource based at least on a result of the decoding of the carrier activation or deactivation message. A UE for performing this method is also provided.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method in a network node for activation or deactivation of a carrier in a communication network supporting carrier aggregation, the method comprising: sending a control signal to schedule a transmission of a carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the control signal comprises an indication of a first resource for a User Equipment (UE) to transmit a feedback message to the network node; sending the carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the carrier activation or deactivation message comprises an indication of a second resource for the UE to transmit the feedback message to the network node; in response to the sending of the activation and deactivation message, receiving the feedback message at the first and second resources; and decoding the feedback message based on power detection at the first and second resources. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving an uplink data packet in response to sending an uplink scheduling grant after the sending of the carrier activation or deactivation message, wherein the received data packet comprises an indication of a carrier state for each configured carrier in the carrier aggregation, which confirms one of: a success and a failure of an operation based on the carrier activation or deactivation message. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the indication of the carrier state is given by a Medium Access Control element. 4. The method of any one of claims 1 to 3, further comprising allocating the second resource prior to sending the carrier activation or deactivation message. 5. The method of claim 4, further comprising querying the UE for an ability to identify the second resource prior to allocating the second resource. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the indication of the second resource is given by setting to 1 a reserved bit R in a Media Access Control element of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein detecting power at the first resource comprises receiving a negative feedback for the carrier activation or deactivation message. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein detecting power at the first resource comprises detecting a power at the first resource higher than a power detected at the second resource. 9. The method of claim 7 or 8, further comprising, in response to detecting power at the first resource, retransmitting a next redundancy version of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein detecting power at the second resource comprises receiving a positive feedback for the carrier activation or deactivation message. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein detecting power at the second resource comprises detecting a power at the second resource higher than a power detected at the first resource. 12. The method of claim 10 or 11, further comprising, in response to detecting power at the second resource, updating a number of activated carriers. 13. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein decoding the feedback message based on power detection at the first and second resources further comprises detecting no power at the first and second resources; and in response to such a detection, retransmitting the control signal and an initial redundancy version of the carrier activation or deactivation message to the UE. 14. The method of any of claims 1-13, wherein the feedback message is a Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) feedback which comprises a bit sequence having bit placeholders only for corresponding activated carriers. 15. A network node comprising a processing circuitry and a network interface connected thereto, the processing circuitry configured to: send a control signal to schedule a transmission of a carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the control signal comprises an indication of a first resource for a User Equipment (UE) to transmit a feedback message to the network node; send the carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the carrier activation or deactivation message comprises an indication of a second resource for the UE to transmit the feedback message to the network node; in response to the sending of the activation and deactivation message, receive the feedback message at the first and second resources; and decode the feedback message based on power detection at the first and second resources. 16. The network node of claim 15, wherein the processing circuitry comprises a processor and a memory connected thereto, the memory comprising instructions that, when executed, cause the processor to perform the steps of sending the control signal, sending the carrier activation or deactivation message, receiving and decoding. 17. The network node of claim 16, wherein the processor is configured to receive an uplink data packet in response to sending an uplink scheduling grant after the sending of the carrier activation or deactivation message, wherein the received data packet comprises an indication of a carrier state for each configured carrier in the carrier aggregation, which confirms one of: a success and a failure of an operation based on the carrier activation or deactivation message. 18. The network node of claim 17, wherein the indication of the carrier state is given by a Medium Access Control element. 19. The network node of any one of claims 16 to 18, wherein the processor is configured to allocate the second resource prior to sending the carrier activation or deactivation message. 20. The network node of claim 19, wherein the processor is configured to query the UE for an ability to identify the second resource prior to allocating the second resource. 21. The network node of any one of claims 15 to 20, wherein the processor is configured to set to 1 a reserved bit R in a Media Access Control element of the carrier activation or deactivation message as the indication of the second resource. 22. The network node of any of claims 16 to 20, wherein the processor is configured to detect power at the first resource, which means that a negative feedback for the carrier activation or deactivation message is received. 23. The network node of claim 22, wherein the processor is configured to further detect a power at the first resource higher than a power at the second resource. 24. The network node of claim 22 or 23, wherein the processor is configured to retransmit a next redundancy version of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 25. The network node of any one of claims 16 to 20, wherein the processor is configured to detect power at the second resource, which means that a positive feedback for the carrier activation or deactivation message is received. 26. The network node of claim 25, wherein the processor is configured to detect a power at the second resource higher than a power at the first resource. 27. The network node of claim 25 or 26, wherein the processor is configured to update a number of activated carriers. 28. The network node of any of claims 16 to 20, wherein the processor is configured to detect no power at the first and second resources; and in response to such a detection, the processor is configured to retransmit the control signal and an initial redundancy version of the carrier activation or deactivation message to the UE. 29. The network node of any one of claims 15-28, wherein the feedback message is a Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) feedback which comprises a bit sequence having bit placeholders only for corresponding activated carriers. 30. A computer program product comprising a non-transitory computer readable storage medium having computer readable program code embodied in the medium, the computer readable program code comprising computer readable code to perform the method of any one or more of claims 1-14. 31. A non- transitory computer readable memory configured to store executable instructions for a network node, the executable instructions when executed by one or more processors cause the network node to perform the method of any one of claims 1 to 14. 32. A method in a User Equipment (UE) for activation or deactivation of a carrier in a communication network supporting carrier aggregation, the method comprising: receiving a control signal for a transmission of a carrier activation or deactivation message command, the control signal including an indication of a first resource for the UE to transmit a feedback message to a network node; receiving a carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the carrier activation or deactivation message comprises an indication of a second resource for the UE to transmit the feedback message to the network node; decoding the received carrier activation or deactivation message; sending the feedback message using one of the first resource and the second resource based at least on a result of the decoding of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 33. The method of claim 32, further comprising, in response to receiving an uplink scheduling grant after receiving the carrier activation or deactivation message from the network node, sending an uplink data packet to the network node, the uplink data packet comprises an indication of a carrier state for each configured carrier for the UE, the carrier state used to confirm one of: a success and failure of an operation based on the carrier activation or deactivation message. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the indication of the carrier state is given by a Medium Access Control element. 35. The method of any one of claims 32-34, wherein the result of the decoding is an unsuccessful decoding of the carrier activation or deactivation message; in response to such a result, transmitting the feedback message to the network node using the first resource. 36. The method of any one of claims 32-35, further comprising failing to identify the indication of the second resource; in response to such a failure, transmitting the feedback message to the network node using the first resource. 37. The method of claim 35 or 36, wherein the feedback message is a negative feedback. 38. The method of claim 37, further comprising receiving a next redundancy version of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 39. The method of claim 36, wherein failing to identify the indication of the second resource comprises detecting that the second resource is unavailable. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein detecting that the second resource is unavailable comprises detecting that a reserved bit R in a Media Access Control element of the carrier activation or deactivation message is set to 0. 41. The method of any one of claims 32 to 34, wherein the result of the decoding is a successful decoding of the carrier activation or deactivation message and a successful identification of the indication of the second resource. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein the successful identification of the indication of the second resource comprises detecting that the second resource is available. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein detecting that the second resource is available comprises detecting that a reserved bit R in a Media Access Control element of the carrier activation or deactivation message is set to 1. 44. The method of any of claims 32-43, wherein the feedback message is a Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) feedback which comprises a bit sequence having bit placeholders only for corresponding activated carriers. 45. A User Equipment (UE) comprising processing circuitry, and a network interface connected thereto, the processing circuitry configured to: receive a control signal for a transmission of a carrier activation or deactivation message command, the control signal including an indication of a first resource for the UE to transmit a feedback message to a network node; receive a carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the carrier activation or deactivation message comprises an indication of a second resource for the UE to transmit the feedback message to the network node; decode the received carrier activation or deactivation message; send the feedback message using one of the first resource and the second resource based at least on a result of the decoding of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 46. The UE of claim 45, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to, in response to receiving an uplink scheduling grant, send an uplink data packet to the network node, the uplink data packet comprises an indication of a carrier state for each configured carrier for the UE, the carrier state used to confirm one of: a success and failure of an operation based on the carrier activation or deactivation message. 47. The UE of claim 46, wherein the indication of the carrier state is given by a Medium Access Control element. 48. The UE of any one of claims 45-47, wherein the processing circuitry fails to decode the activation or deactivation message; and wherein, in response to such a result, the processing circuitry is configured to transmit the feedback message to the network node using the first resource. 49. The UE of any one of claims 45-48, wherein the processing circuitry fails to identify the indication of the second resource; and wherein, in response to such a failure, the processing circuitry is configured to transmit the feedback message to the network node using the first resource. 50. The UE of claim 48 or 49, wherein the feedback message is a negative feedback. 51. The UE of claim 50, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to receive a next redundancy version of the carrier activation or deactivation message. 52. The UE of claim 49, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to detect that the second resource is unavailable. 53. The UE of claim 52, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to detect that a reserved bit R in a Media Access Control element of the carrier activation or deactivation message is set to 0. 54. The UE of any one of claims 45 to 47, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to successfully decode the carrier activation or deactivation message and successfully identify the indication of the second resource. 55. The UE of claim 54, wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to detect that the second resource is available. 56. The UE of claim 55, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to detect that a reserved bit R in a Media Access Control element of the carrier activation or deactivation message is set to 1. 57. The UE of any of claims 45-56, wherein the feedback message is a Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) feedback which comprises a bit sequence having bit placeholders only for corresponding activated carriers. 58. A computer program product comprising a non-transitory computer readable storage medium having computer readable program code embodied in the medium, the computer readable program code comprising computer readable code to perform any one or more of claims 32-44. 59. A non-transitory computer readable memory configured to store executable instructions for a User Equipment (UE), the executable instructions when executed by one or more processors cause the UE to perform any of claims 32-44. 60. A wireless device (WD) comprising: a receiver configured to receive a control signal for a transmission of a carrier activation or deactivation message command, the control signal including an indication of a first resource for the UE to transmit a feedback message to a network node and to receive a carrier activation or deactivation message; wherein the carrier activation or deactivation message comprises an indication of a second resource for the UE to transmit the feedback message to the network node; processing circuitry configured to decode the received carrier activation or deactivation message; a transmitter configured to send the feedback message using one of the first resource and the second resource based at least on a result of the decoding of the carrier activation or deactivation message; and power supply circuitry configured to supply power to said wireless device.
